| father = | mother = | spouse = | education = | alma_mater = | relatives = [[Sam Tomkins]] (brother)
[[Logan Tomkins]] (brother) }} }} '''Joel Andrew Tomkins''' (born 21 March 1987) is an English professional rugby coach and former professional player. He was a [[List of dual-code rugby internationals|dual-code international]], having played for both the [[England national rugby league team|England national rugby league]] as a {{rlp|sr}} forward and the [[England national rugby union team|rugby union team]] at [[centre (rugby union)|outside centre]].{{cite web | url=http://www.catalansdragons.com/en/articles-9/289-12141-assistant-coaches-appointed/ | title=Assistant coaches appointed | date=October 2024 }} After On 21 May 2025, Tomkins took over as interim coach of [[Super League]] side [[Catalans Dragons]], following Steve McNamara. On 14 April 2026, it was announced that Tomkins had left the club due to personal reasons after just 9 months in charge.{{cite web| url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cdxkke29245o|title=Head coach Tomkins leaves Catalans Dragons|accessdate=14 April 2026}} Thereafter, Tomkins signed on to be a defensive coach at [[Gloucester_Rugby|Gloucester]] in rugby union. https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/joel-tomkins-gloucester-appointment-april-2026 ==Background== Tomkins was born in [[Warrington]], [[Cheshire]], England, but he was raised in [[Wigan]] in [[Greater Manchester]]. He is the eldest brother of [[Sam Tomkins]] and [[Logan Tomkins]], who also both played rugby league. ==Club career== ===Early career=== Tomkins is a former [[St. John Fisher Catholic High School (Wigan)|St. John Fisher]] player, where he played in the [[Wigan Warriors]]' Academy at under-18's level in 2003. He was selected for the 2004 Academy Origin Series. He primarily plays in the {{rlp|sr}}, but he can also play as a {{rlp|ce}}, {{rlp|lf}} and {{rlp|pr}}. ===Wigan Warriors (2005–11)=== In 2005, he was called up into the first-team at the age of 18 and he made his senior début for the [[Wigan Warriors]] against the [[Widnes Vikings]] in March 2005. During 2005, he made another 12 appearances and scoring a total of 3 tries in the process. The talented youngster was given his chance at first-team level under coach [[Ian Millward]], who had become Head Coach of the [[Wigan Warriors]] in 2005. Tomkins started the first match of the 2006 season at {{rlp|lf}}, in a match against the [[Catalans Dragons]] which the [[Wigan Warriors]] lost, 38–30. He was demoted to the bench for the second match of the season against the [[Leeds Rhinos]]. After 17-minutes he was brought from off the bench and scored a try with his first touch of the ball to give the [[Wigan Warriors]] a 4–0 lead. The [[Wigan Warriors]] went onto lose the match, 16–24. In February 2006, Tomkins agreed a contract extension with the [[Wigan Warriors]] which would keep him at the club until 2008. He started from the bench for the next two matches against the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and the [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], but he was dropped to the Academy just shortly after. He later returned to the squad when he was named on the bench for the [[Challenge Cup]] fourth round match, against the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] on 2 April 2006. He also played in the next couple of [[Super League]] matches against the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] and [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], before again being dropped to the Academy. Team Head Coach [[Ian Millward]] was sacked in April 2006, because of the poor results from the [[Wigan Warriors]] side. He was replaced by [[Brian Noble (rugby league)|Brian Noble]] on 20 April 2006. The [[Wigan Warriors]] were bottom of the [[Super League]] and [[Brian Noble (rugby league)|Noble]] decided to play a more experienced squad, which meant that Tomkins would not make another appearance during 2006. [[File:Joel Tomkins Wigan.jpg|thumb|right|Tomkins playing for the [[Wigan Warriors]] during his first-spell at the club in 2007]] Tomkins started the 2007 season in the [[Wigan Warriors]]' Senior Academy, where he was in good-form scoring 5 tries in one match against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]' Academy. But despite this he was not called up into the first-team squad until 18 May 2007, for the [[Super League]] match against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. Tomkins suffered an ankle injury during the match and was out for 2-weeks, before returning to the [[Wigan Warriors]]' Senior Academy. He then later returned to the [[Wigan Warriors]] first-team for the match against the [[Catalans Dragons]] on 15 June 2007. ====Widnes Vikings (loan)==== On 27 July 2007, the 20-year-old joined the [[Widnes Vikings]], initially on a month's long loan-deal, with a view to extending the deal to the end of the season. ====Return to Wigan==== He became a regular first-team player for the Wigan Warriors during the 2008 season, he was a substitute in the first match of the season against [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins RL]]. He was not included in the Wigan squad for the next two games against the [[Castleford Tigers]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]], but he returned to the squad as a substitute for the match against the [[Bradford Bulls]] in which he scored his first try in the 2008 season. Despite a good performance against the [[Bradford Bulls]], he was not included in the squad for the next two [[Super League]] games, his place in the squad was taken by [[Australians|Australian]] {{rlp|sr}}, [[Phil Bailey]]. His next appearance was in the [[Good Friday]] derby match against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] as a substitute. He kept his place in the squad for 13 consecutive games and he scored two more tries, one against the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]] round 14 and the other against the [[Catalans Dragons]] in round 17. Tomkins continued to be a presence in the [[Wigan Warriors]]' 2009 [[Super League]] season. He was successful in the 2010 [[Super League]] season, lifting the [[League Leaders' Shield]] with the [[Wigan Warriors]]. Tomkins played in the [[2010 Super League Grand Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite news|last=Wilson|first=Andy|title=Wigan turn St Helens into sinners with Super League Grand Final win|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/oct/02/wigan-st-helens-grand-final|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=guardian.co.uk|date=2 October 2010}} He was then part of the [[Wigan Warriors]]' side that lost the [[2011 World Club Challenge]] to the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]]. Tomkins made five consecutive appearances at the start of the 2011 Super League season, including the opening fixture against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], before being sent-off in a game against [[Hull F.C.]]. He received a two-match ban for the incident with his brother Sam also banned for one-game.{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=St Helens 16–16 Wigan|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/9393298.stm|access-date=14 May 2011|newspaper=BBC|date=12 February 2011}}{{cite news|last=Wilson|first=Andy|title=Wigan Warriors make brave attempt but Dragons are still worlds apart|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/feb/27/wigan-st-george-illawarra-world-club-challenge|access-date=14 May 2011|newspaper=The Guardian|date=27 February 2011}}{{cite news|title=Wigan Warriors 14–12 Hull FC|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/9417484.stm|access-date=14 May 2011|newspaper=BBC|date=13 March 2011}}{{cite news|last=Higham|first=Paul|title=Wigan lose Tomkins brothers|url=http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12040_6815697,00.html|access-date=14 May 2011|newspaper=Sky Sports|date=15 March 2011}} Tomkins returned and scored his first try of the season in round 8, against the [[Leeds Rhinos]].{{cite news|title=Leeds Rhinos 22–22 Wigan Warriors|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/9439007.stm|access-date=14 May 2011|newspaper=BBC|date=1 April 2011}} His next tries came against [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in rounds 10 and 12.{{cite news|title=Hull KR 16–28 Wigan|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/9455872.stm|access-date=14 May 2011|newspaper=BBC|date=15 April 2011}} Tomkins played as a {{rlp|ce}} in the [[2011 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium]]. He scored a try after his brother Sam sent him away down the wing to score.{{cite news|last=Scott|first=Ged|title=Leeds 18–28 Wigan|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14670204|access-date=21 November 2019|newspaper=[[BBC Sport]]|date=27 August 2011}}{{cite news| url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/aug/27/leeds-rhinos-wigan-warriors-challenge-cup | location=London | work=The Guardian | first=Andy | last=Wilson | title=Leeds Rhinos 18–28 Wigan Warriors – Challenge Cup final match report | date=27 August 2011}} Tomkins fended Leeds' {{rlp|sh}} [[Danny McGuire]] off and he side-stepped past [[Brent Webb]] and [[Carl Ablett]] to score under the posts, after an eighty-metre sprint. Wigan won the game 28–18, giving Tomkins his first [[Challenge Cup]] 'Winners' Medal.' [[File:Joel Tomkins Warrington Vs. Wigan Scrap.jpg|thumb|right|Tomkins playing for the Wigan Warriors against the [[Warrington Wolves]] in 2011]] ===Saracens (2011–14)=== At the end of the [[2011 Super League season]], Tomkins switched rugby codes after agreeing a £400,000 contract with [[Saracens F.C.|Saracens]], after a £250,000 release-fee was agreed with the Wigan.{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-union/15620047 | title=Joel Tomkins switches codes from Wigan to Saracens | publisher=BBC | access-date=25 January 2025 | date=7 November 2011}} Tomkins played as an outside centre in rugby union, making his debut in a [[Anglo-Welsh Cup|LV= Cup]] win over [[Worcester Warriors]] in January 2012, scoring two tries.{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-union/16783411 | title=Saracens newcomer Joel Tomkins bags two tries on rugby union debut | publisher=BBC | access-date=25 January 2025 | date=29 January 2012}} He also scored on his [[Heineken Cup]] debut against [[Edinburgh Rugby|Edinburgh]] at [[Murrayfield Stadium|Murrayfield]] in October 2012,{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-union/19874388 | title=Heineken Cup: Edinburgh 0-45 Saracens | publisher=BBC | access-date=25 January 2025 | date=13 October 2012}} and played in the semi-final defeat to [[RC Toulon]] at [[Twickenham Stadium|Twickenham]] the following April.{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-union/22288857 | title=Heineken Cup semi-final: Saracens 12-24 Toulon | publisher=BBC | access-date=25 January 2025 | date=28 April 2013}} [[File:Joel Tomkins Saracens.jpg|thumb|right|Tomkins running towards the [[Harlequin F.C.|Harlequins]] line in 2012]] ===Return to Wigan Warriors (2014–18)=== On 18 June 2014, the Wigan Warriors announced that they had re-signed Tomkins with immediate effect.{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/27914844 | title=Joel Tomkins rejoins Wigan Warriors from Saracens | publisher=BBC Sport | date=18 June 2014 | access-date=25 January 2025}} He played in the [[2014 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by St Helens at [[Old Trafford]].{{cite web|url = https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/11156623/St-Helens-14-Wigan-Warriors-6-Moment-of-madness-from-Wales-international-Ben-Flower-costs-Wigan-dear.html|title= St Helens 14 Wigan Warriors 6: Moment of madness from Wales international Ben Flower costs Wigan dear|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Daily Telegraph|access-date=15 October 2014}}{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/11/st-helens-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title= St Helens win Grand Final after Wigan's Ben Flower is sent off|date= 11 October 2014 |work=Guardian |access-date=15 October 2014}}{{cite web|url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/29559845|title= St Helens 14–6 Wigan Warriors|date= 11 October 2014 |work=BBC Sport|access-date=15 October 2014}} He played in the [[2015 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the Leeds Rhinos at Old Trafford.{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2015/oct/10/leeds-wigan-super-league-grand-final-match-report|title=Leeds pip Wigan to seal treble after brilliant, breathless Grand Final|work=Guardian|date=10 October 2015|access-date=8 November 2019}} Tomkins was victorious in his third consecutive grand final in [[2016 Super League Grand Final|2016]]. He also claimed victory in the [[2017 World Club Challenge]], against the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] by a score of 22–6. Tomkins' final game for the [[Wigan Warriors]] came in a 23–0 [[Challenge Cup]] quarter-final defeat on 2 June 2018, by the [[Warrington Wolves]].{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/jun/02/warrington-wigan-challenge-cup-match-report | title=Tyrone Roberts drives Warrington past Wigan into Challenge Cup semi-finals | publisher=The Observer | first=Aaron | last=Bower | date=2 June 2018 | access-date=25 January 2025}} On 4 June 2018, Tomkins was banned from playing for four weeks and fined £10,000, after footage posted on [[social media]] went viral of him abusing bar staff in a Wigan public house. His brother Sam was also fined £5,000 for his more limited role in the incident. Tomkins himself tendered his resignation to Wigan Warriors' rugby general manager [[Kris Radlinski]], which was accepted by all parties involved.{{cite news | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/jun/03/wigan-suspend-joel-tomkins-abusive-rant-sam-tomkins | title=Wigan suspend Joel Tomkins for abusive rant at barmaid | work=The Guardian | first=Aaron | last=Bower | date=3 June 2018 | access-date=2 June 2025 }} ===Hull Kingston Rovers (2018–19)=== Following his resignation from Wigan, Tomkins signed an 18-month contract to play for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] at [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]] until the end of the 2019 seasom, despite interest from other rival [[Super League]] clubs.{{cite news | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/jun/11/joel-tomkins-resigns-wigan-joins-hull-kr-super-league | title=Joel Tomkins quits Wigan for Hull KR after 'embarrassing' bar incident | work=The Guardian | first=Aaron | last=Bower | date=11 June 2018 | access-date=25 January 2025 }} On 17 June 2018, Tomkins made his Hull KR Super League début in a 24–24 draw, against the [[Castleford Tigers]] at the [[Mend-A-Hose Jungle]]. On 29 June 2018, Joel bagged his first try for Hull Kingston Rovers on his home début at [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]] against the [[Huddersfield Giants]], in a 37–10 [[Super League]] victory. It was revealed on 24 November 2018, that Tomkins had penned a new three-year contract extension to remain at Hull Kingston Rovers until at least the end of the 2021 season. It was revealed on 11 January 2019, that Joel was appointed as captain at Hull Kingston Rovers ahead of the start of the 2019 Super League season.{{cite web|date=11 January 2019|title=Tomkins named as captain|url=http://mobile.twitter.com/hullkrofficial/status/1083800152429785089}} [[File:Joel Tomkins Hull KR.jpg|thumb|right|Tomkins walking out for Hull KR in 2019]] In October 2019, he signed a two-year deal to join [[Catalans Dragons]] starting in 2020.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/50117694|title=Catalans sign ex-Hull KR and Wigan second-row on two-year deal|work=BBC Sport}} ===Catalans Dragons (2020–21)=== During Catalans elimination final victory over Leeds in the 2020 Super League playoffs, Tomkins was placed on report after inappropriate contact with Leeds player [[Richie Myler]].{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12216/12134189/catalans-dragons-michael-mcilorum-and-joel-tomkins-facing-lengthy-bans/|title=Catalans Dragons' Michael McIlorum and Joel Tomkins facing lengthy bans|website=www.skysports.com}} On 18 November, Tomkins was suspended for eight matches and fined £500 after he was found guilty of inappropriate contact.{{cite web|url=https://superleague.co.uk/article/1736/tomkins-banned-for-eight-matches/|title=Tomkins banned for eight matches|website=www.superleague.co.uk|access-date=18 November 2020|archive-date=5 December 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201205083907/https://www.superleague.co.uk/article/1736/tomkins-banned-for-eight-matches|url-status=dead}} On 9 October 2021, Tomkins played for Catalans in their [[2021 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against St. Helens.{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58858850|title=Super League Grand Final: Catalans Dragons 10-12 St Helens: Saints win three in a row|work=BBC Sport}} === Retirement === On 18 November 2021, Tomkins announced his retirement from professional rugby, after initially signing a contract to join [[RFL Championship|Championship]] club [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh Centurions]]{{Cite news|title=Joel Tomkins: Former England back-rower retires and will not join Leigh Centurions|language=en-GB|work=BBC Sport|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/59335266|access-date=25 January 2025|date=18 November 2021}}{{cite web | url=https://www.totalrl.com/five-ex-super-league-players-who-bizarrely-never-played-a-game-for-the-club-they-signed-for/ | title=Five ex-Super League players who bizarrely never played a game for the club they signed for | first=Callum | last=Walker | date=24 January 2025 | access-date=25 January 2025 | publisher=Total Rugby League}} for the [[2022 RFL Championship|2022 season]]. ==Coaching== ===Catalans Dragons=== On 21 May 2025, Tomkins was appointed as interim head-coach for [[Catalans Dragons]] in the Super League following the sudden departure of [[Steve McNamara]],{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=21 May 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Sam Tomkins confirms Catalans coaching plans as Leeds link to Steve McNamara addressed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/sam-tomkins-confirms-catalans-coaching-plans-as-leeds-link-to-steve-mcnamara-addressed}} who was sacked a month later.[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c77vvx5vj3xo bbc.co.uk] In July, he was appointed as the permanent head coach, having his contract extended to the end of the 2027 season.{{cite web |title=Head coach Tomkins extends Catalans deal until 2027 |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cp3kvd6ypywo |publisher=BBC Sport |date=22 July 2025}} On 10 April 2026 it was reported that he had departed [[Catalans Dragons]] due to personal circumstances, with [[Ryan Sheridan (rugby league)|Ryan Sheridan]] taking temporary charge{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=11 Apr 2026|author=Kasey Smith|title=Ryan Sheridan rues 'awful' Catalans Dragons start to quarter-final|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ryan-sheridan-rues-awful-catalans-dragons-start-to-quarter-final/}} ===Gloucester Rugby=== On 21 April 2026, it was announced that Tomkins would take up the role of defensive coach at [[Gloucester Rugby|Gloucester RFC]] for the 2026-27 [[rugby union]] [[Premiership Rugby]] season{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=21 Apr 2026|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Joel Tomkins’ next move confirmed following Catalans departure with code switch|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/joel-tomkins-gloucester-appointment-april-2026}} ==International career== ===England rugby league=== Tomkins made his senior international debut for [[England national rugby league team|England]] in 2010, a mid-season test against [[France national rugby league team|France]].{{cite news | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/jun/12/england-france-rugby-league|title=Record-equalling Sam Tomkins scores four tries for England as France flop|first=Andy|last=Wilson|date=12 June 2010|access-date=25 January 2025|work=The Guardian}}{{cite news | last=Hadfield | first=Dave | title=Widdop passes his first Test at a canter | work=The Independent | place=UK | publisher=Independent Print Limited | date = 13 June 2010 | url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/widdop-passes-his-first-test-at--a-canter-1999087.html | access-date=25 January 2025}} Tomkins was an interchange forward in a game notable for his brother Sam scoring four tries, and would be Joel's only international appearance against a national team on home soil. In autumn, the brothers were selected for the [[2010 Rugby League Four Nations]] in [[Australia]] and [[New Zealand]].{{cite news | url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/england-four-nations-squad-revealed | title=England Four Nations Squad Revealed | publisher=Love Rugby League | date=4 October 2010 | access-date=25 January 2025}} Tomkins played in a pre-tournament draw against [[New Zealand Maori rugby league team|New Zealand Maori]],{{cite news | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/oct/16/england-new-zealand-maori | title=England unable to beat New Zealand Maori but injuries are main concern | first=Andy | last=Wilson | work=The Guardian | access-date=25 January 2025 | date=16 October 2010}} before featuring in both losses to [[New Zealand national rugby league team|New Zealand]]{{cite news | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/oct/23/new-zealand-england-four-nations | title=New Zealand put ponderous England to the sword | first=Andy | last=Wilson | work=The Guardian | access-date=25 January 2025 | date=23 October 2010}} and [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]].{{cite news | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/oct/31/luke-lewis-england-out | title=England out of Four Nations after conclusive defeat by Australia | first=Andy | last=Wilson | work=The Guardian | access-date=25 January 2025 | date=16 October 2010}} In 2011, Tomkins was selected for England in the inaugural [[2011 International Origin|International Origin]] against a selection of non-English Super League players. Tomkins started from the bench but scored his first international try, as the home side lost 12-16.{{cite news | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/jun/10/england-george-carmont-exiles | title=England sunk at the last as George Carmont try seals Exiles' triumph | first=Andy | last=Wilson | work=The Guardian | access-date=25 January 2025 | date=11 June 2011}}{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/13696752 | title=George Carmont snatches Exiles victory at Headingley | publisher=BBC Sport | access-date=25 January 2025 | date=10 June 2011}} At the end of the season, Tomkins asked to be omitted from England's [[2011 Rugby League Four Nations]] squad for personal reasons, ultimately switching codes while the tournament was in progress.{{cite news | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/10/england-rugby-league-team-four-nations | title=Sam Tomkins commits to England as brother prepares for union switch | first=Andy | last=Wilson | work=The Guardian | access-date=25 January 2025 | date=10 October 2011}}{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-union/15620047 | title=Joel Tomkins switches codes from Wigan to Saracens | publisher=BBC Sport | access-date=25 January 2025 | date=7 November 2011}} After returning from rugby union, Tomkins returned to the England squad for the [[2014 Rugby League Four Nations]], again in Australia and New Zealand.{{cite news | title=Four Nations: Sean O'Loughlin named England captain | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/29497061 | date=5 October 2014 | access-date=25 January 2025 | publisher=BBC Sport}} He started all three matches in the tournament,{{cite news | title=England come up just short in Four Nations defeat against New Zealand | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/nov/08/england-new-zealand-four-nations-report | first=Steve | last=Mascord | work=The Guardian |date=25 October 2014 | access-date=25 January 2025}}{{cite news | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/nov/02/australia-squeeze-past-england-to-make-four-nations-final- | title=Australia squeeze past England to keep Four Nations dream alive | first=Steve | last=Mascord | work=The Guardian | date=1 November 2014 | access-date=25 January 2025}} and scored his second international try against [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] in the opening game at [[Suncorp Stadium]].{{cite news | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2014/oct/25/england-beat-samoa-four-nations|title=England survive Samoa scare to secure victory in Four Nations opener|first=Steve|last=Mascord|work=The Guardian|date=25 October 2014|access-date=25 January 2025}} ===England rugby union=== ====England Saxons==== In 2013, Tomkins was called up for the first of his two caps for the second-string [[England Saxons national rugby union team|England Saxons]] [[rugby union]] team.{{citation|publisher=Rugby Football Union|title=Player Profile}} {{cite news| url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/jan/22/joel-tomkins-kyle-eastmond-england-saxons | first=Paul | last=Rees | title=Joel Tomkins and Kyle Eastmond set to start for England Saxons | date=22 January 2013 | access-date=25 January 2025}} Starting at outside centre in both tests, Tomkins enjoyed initial success against the [[Ireland Wolfhounds]] in a closely contested match, ending in a 14–10 victory for the [[England Saxons national rugby union team|England Saxons]].{{cite news | publisher=BBC Sport | title = Ireland Wolfhounds 10–14 England Saxons | date=25 January 2013 | access-date=25 January 2025 | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/21147198}} However a week later, Tomkins experienced his first taste of defeat in a union white shirt by a strong [[Scotland A national rugby union team|Scotland A team]], who ran-out winners 9–13, in a rain-swept match set in [[Newcastle upon Tyne|Newcastle]].{{cite news | publisher=BBC Sport | title=England Saxons 9–13 Scotland A | date=1 February 2023 | access-date=25 January 2025 | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/21300404}} ====Senior==== Following Tomkins' good-form for the [[Saracens F.C.|Saracens]] in the 2013–2014 season and an injury to [[Manu Tuilagi]], Tomkins was selected for the [[England national rugby union team|England]] elite player squad for the [[2013 end-of-year rugby union internationals|2013 Autumn International Series]].{{cite news | work=The Guardian | title= Joel Tomkins among four changes in England rugby squad | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/16/joel-tomkins-england-rugby-squad | date=16 October 2023 | access-date= 25 January 2025 | first=Paul | last=Rees}} He won his début cap in a 20–13 victory over [[Australia national rugby union team|Australia]],{{cite news | title=England 20–13 Australia | publisher=BBC Sport | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-union/24779192 | first=Tom | last=Fordyce | date=3 November 2013 | access-date=25 January 2025}} playing the full 80 minutes to regain the [[Cook Cup]]. The following week, Tomkins again played for the duration against [[Argentina national rugby union team|Argentina]],{{cite news | url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/nov/09/england-argentina-autumn-internationals | work=The Guardian | title=England do enough to beat Argentina despite poor second-half display | first=Paul | last=Rees | date=9 November 2013 | access-date=25 January 2025}} before leaving the field injured with four minutes remaining against the [[New Zealand national rugby union team|All Blacks]] in his third and final appearance for England.{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-union/24949390 | title=England lose 30-22 to New Zealand at Twickenham | first=Bryn | last=Palmer | date=16 November 2013 | access-date=25 January 2025 | publisher=BBC Sport}} ==Career statistics== ===Rugby league=== {|class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;" |- style="background:#bdb76b;" ! Season !! Team !! Apps !! Tries !! Goals !! DG !!
